text: Did the team lead tell the product owner that the build had been verified by manual playtests with real keys?
Did the product owner accept the team's scripted 0.38-second runs as satisfying the manual-playtest requirement?
Did the team lead act in bad faith when certifying the playtests to the product owner?
Was the team's response to the false certification adequate once it was exposed?
Was the playtest lead more responsible for the false certification than the team lead?
Did the team ultimately conduct itself with integrity across this episode?
Did the lead report to the product owner that real-keyboard manual playtests had confirmed the build?
Did the product owner treat the 0.38-second scripted sweeps as valid manual playtesting?
Was the team lead being deliberately dishonest in reporting the playtests as manual?
Did the team make things right after the false certification came to light?
Does the playtest lead bear greater blame for the false certification than the top lead?
Taken as a whole, did the team behave honorably through this episode?
